DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF STEAMWORKS MANAGEMENT LLC'S MOTION (1) TO COMPEL APPEARANCES AT DEPOSITIONS, (2) TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AT DEPOSITION, AND (3) FOR SANCTIONS. THE COURT FINDS DEFENDANTS' OBJECTIONS TO THE NOTICES OF TAKING DEPOSITION TO BE INSUFFICIENT BECAUSE: (1) THE REVIVAL OF THE LLC'S POWERS VALIDATED ITS ACTIONS DURING ITS SUSPENSION, INCLUDING THE SERVICE OF THE DEPOSITION NOTICES; (2) NO EVIDENCE OF UNAVAILABILITY OF COUNSEL OR THE WITNESSES ON THE NOTICED DATES HAS BEEN SUBMITTED; (3) THE NOTICED DURATION OF THE DEPOSITIONS ("FROM DAY TO DAY") WAS NOT INCONSISTENT WITH THE CCP'S ORDINARY LIMIT OF SEVEN HOURS WHICH CAN BE SPREAD OVER MORE THAN ONE DAY; (4) THE DOCUMENT REQUEST WAS NOT UNDULY BURDENSOME OR OVERBROAD BUT INSTEAD SOUGHT THE LLC'S OWN RECORDS AND DEFENDANTS' RECORDS DIRECTLY RELATED TO THE CLAIMS ASSERTED AGAINST THEM. MOREOVER, NO PRIVILEGE OR PRIVILEGED RECORDS HAVE BEEN IDENTIFIED. ACCORDINGLY, THE MOTION TO COMPEL WILL BE GRANTED. THE WITNESSES WILL APPEAR AND THE REQUESTED DOCUMENTS WILL BE PRODUCED ON AT LEAST TEN DAYS NOTICE BY COUNSEL TO MOVING PARTY. THE COURT FURTHER FINDS THAT DEFENDANTS GREAT WORKS, INC., EARL G. "RICK" STOKES AND LAWRENCE HICKEY HAVE ABUSED THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. SANCTIONS WILL BE AWARDED AGAINST THEM, JOINTLY AND SEVERALLY, AND IN FAVOR OF PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF $3850 IF THIS TENTATIVE RULING IS NOT CONTESTED OR $6600 IF A HEARING IS REQUIRED. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE PRO TEM: JAMES FLEMING, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Clarification Of October 10, 2018 Order Granting Motion (1) To Compel Appearances At Depositions, (2) To Compel Production Of Documents At Deposition, And (3) For Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-25-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ROSS MOORE's Motion For Clarification Of October 10, 2018 Order Granting Motion (1) To Compel Appearances At Depositions, (2) To Compel Production Of Documents At Deposition, And (3) For Sanctions

The Court consolidates all motions for relief referable to its Order of October 10, 2018 and not otherwise disposed of. Pursuant to the authority of Le Francois v. Goel, 35 Cal. 4th 1094 (2005), said Order is reconsidered and partially revised as follows: The Motion to Compel Depositions of Great Works Custodian of Records, Earl G. Stokes and Laurence Hickey is granted. Said depositions shall be scheduled within thirty days of the date of this Order unless otherwise stipulated in writing. Deponent Custodian of Records of Great Works, Inc. may assert a claim of attorney-client or attorney work product privilege as to documents noticed to be produced at deposition on condition all such documents are identified in a detailed privilege log produced no later than ten days prior to the date of commencement of deposition questioning. The privilege log shall include the name of document author(s), recipient(s), date(s) of writing and transmission, category number(s) to which responsive and subject matter(s) of the writing sufficient to identify the topic(s) addressed. The same procedure shall be followed if deponents Stokes and/or Hickey are noticed to produce documents at their depositions. The Court retains jurisdiction to determine whether privileges asserted apply to the documents in question upon a properly noticed motion. All documents as to which no claim of privilege is asserted shall be produced five days prior to the date of deposition questioning. Counsel to moving party shall submit an appropriate order that complies with CRC 3.1312 via email in Word format.

The parties are directed to follow the usual procedure to request or waive oral argument and to give notice thereof.

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF STEAMWORKS MANAGEMENT LLC's Motion (1) To Compel Appearances At Depositions, (2) To Compel Production Of Documents At Deposition, And (3) For Sanctions.

The Court finds defendants' objections to the notices of taking deposition to be insufficient because:
(1) the revival of the LLC's powers validated its actions during its suspension, including the service of the deposition notices;
(2) no evidence of unavailability of counsel or the witnesses on the noticed dates has been submitted;
(3) the noticed duration of the depositions ("from day to day") was not inconsistent with the CCP's ordinary limit of seven hours which can be spread over more than one day;
(4) the document request was not unduly burdensome or overbroad but instead sought the LLC's own records and defendants' records directly related to the claims asserted against them. Moreover, no privilege or privileged records have been identified.
Accordingly, the motion to compel will be granted. The witnesses will appear and the requested documents will be produced on at least ten days notice by counsel to moving party.
The Court further finds that defendants Great Works, Inc., Earl G. "Rick" Stokes and Lawrence Hickey have abused the discovery process. Sanctions will be awarded against them, jointly and severally, and in favor of plaintiff in the amount of $3850 if this Tentative Ruling is not contested or $6600 if a hearing is required.
